Optimize book lookups: reverse linear search + FrozenDictionary

- BookSide: Replace binary search with reverse linear scan from end (best price).
  82% of real market data operations hit the top 5 price levels (avg distance 3.6),
  making linear scan 1-4 comparisons vs binary search's 7-8 for typical books.
  Falls back to binary search for books with >64 price levels.

- BookManager: Use FrozenDictionary<ulong, OrderBook> for the books collection
  after snapshot completes. Optimized hash layout for 18K instruments that are
  looked up ~33M times during RealTime processing. Mutable dictionary used during
  setup (InstrDef + Snapshot), frozen after OnSnapshotComplete.

- Remove temporary profiling instrumentation (Stopwatch in RunSyncLoop, BookManager).

Profiling data that motivated these changes:
  SBE parse: ~0.04 µs/msg (near zero, as expected for zero-copy overlay)
  Book ops: ~0.33 µs/op (10.5s total, 34% of processing time)
  TryReceive (mmap + PQ): 12.1s (39%)
  Price level search: 31.5M searches, avg 3.6 levels from end, 82% within top 5

commit.message:
Reduce code-review skill token usage (~29% smaller) (#126779)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was generated with the assistance of Copilot.

## Reduce code-review skill token usage (~29% smaller)

We've been seeing several 20-minute timeouts during code review runs. To
my knowledge this is a hard limit for GitHub agentic workflows. The
`code-review` SKILL.md is the largest skill file in dotnet/runtime at
~68KB (~17K tokens), and reducing its size should help the model work
more efficiently within the available time. This PR removes ~20KB (~5K
tokens) of low-value content without losing any review rules or
procedures.

### Changes

**1. Remove developer quote examples (−19KB)**

The skill contained ~150 indented blockquote lines (`> "..."`) with real
maintainer review quotes used as phrasing examples under each rule
bullet. These illustrated *how* to phrase feedback but did not define
*what* to check. All actual review rules, severity guidance, and
actionable instructions are preserved.

**2. Extract API Approval Verification into a separate file (−3KB from
always-loaded context)**

The API Approval Verification procedure is a self-contained
sub-procedure only needed when a PR introduces new public API surface.
It is now in `api-approval-check.md` alongside SKILL.md, loaded on
demand.

- Step 0 and Step 2 both contain explicit **MUST-load** instructions
with the exact file path
- A fail-closed clause ensures that if the file cannot be loaded, the
agent reports an error and blocks the review rather than silently
skipping verification
- The extracted file is fully self-contained with all three verification
steps

**3. Tweaked one line to note that always initialize is a managed only
rule**

### Impact

| Metric | Before | After | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| SKILL.md size | 68.9 KB | 48.7 KB | −29.3% |
| Always-loaded tokens | ~17.2K | ~12.2K | −5K |
| Review rules/procedures | All | All | None removed |
| API verification | Inline | On-demand file | Loaded when needed |

### Context: skill sizes in dotnet/skills

For reference, the 78 SKILL.md files in `dotnet/skills` average ~10.8KB
(~2,700 tokens), with the largest at ~36KB (~9,000 tokens). The runtime
code-review skill at 48.7KB post-trim is still larger than any of those,
but it covers a comprehensive multi-step review procedure rather than a
single-purpose task.

---------

commit.message:
Initial commit: .NET remote Whisper transcription server

Minimal ASP.NET Web API (net10.0) that provides remote Whisper AI
transcription for OpenScanner. Shells out to whisper-cli from whisper.cpp.

Endpoints:
- POST /transcribe: accepts multipart WAV upload + optional prompt,
  returns JSON with transcribed text
- GET /health: returns server status and model info

Features:
- Configurable whisper binary, model path, and timeout via appsettings.json
- Serialized transcription requests to avoid overloading
- Radio-context default prompt matching OpenScanner local service
- Blank audio detection and filtering

commit.message:
Fix channel routing: keep all effects in global chain

WASM engines (LibopenmptEngine, FurnaceDispatch, SunVox, SID, etc.) bypass
channelOutputs entirely — audio goes directly from worklet to masterInput/synthBus.
The mixer creates channelOutput entries for volume/pan/mute control, but no
signal flows through them. The previous parallel send approach connected to
these empty shells, removing the effect from the global chain and placing it
in a dead-end send — causing the effect to disappear.

Fix: All effects always go through the global master effects chain regardless
of selectedChannels. The channel preference is stored in the config for future
use when proper per-channel separation is implemented. The broken parallel
send architecture is bypassed (teardown called, no new sends created).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix channel routing: fall back to global chain when channelOutputs unavailable

WASM engines (LibopenmptEngine, FurnaceDispatch, SunVox, SID, etc.) bypass
the per-channel channelOutputs system — their audio goes directly from the
WASM worklet to masterInput. When a user routed an effect to specific channels,
the effect was removed from the global chain but couldn't connect to any
channel outputs, resulting in the effect being in NEITHER chain (no effect).

Fix: ChannelRoutedEffects.rebuild() now pre-checks whether channel outputs
actually exist. If no channels have audio nodes (WASM playback), the effect
ID is NOT returned in the successfully-routed list. ToneEngine.rebuildMasterEffects()
then falls those effects back into the global chain so they still process audio.

This means:
- During WASM module playback: channel selection is stored but effect stays global
- During native synth playback: channel routing works via parallel sends
- No more silent/missing effects when clicking channel buttons

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add SF2 live editing engine with C64 memory bridge

SF2Engine wraps C64SIDEngine (websid backend) providing:
- readRAM/writeRAM on ScriptNodePlayerEngine → C64SIDEngine → SF2Engine
- High-level edit ops: writeInstrumentByte, writeCommandByte,
  writeTableByte, writeSequence, writeOrderEntry
- Sequence packer/unpacker: SF2SeqEvent[] ↔ packed C64 memory format
- Flight recorder: captures 25 SID registers per rAF frame (2048 buffer)
- SID register reading: readSIDRegisters, readSIDVoice

Integration:
- NativeEngineRouting detects sf2StoreData on song, creates SF2Engine
  with driver info (memory map, table defs) for live editing
- TrackerReplayer stores SF2Engine ref, getSF2Engine() accessor
- useSF2Engine hook: acquires engine ref, polls SID regs at 20Hz
- useSF2LiveSync hook: subscribes to store edits (instruments, orders,
  sequences) and pushes changes to C64 RAM immediately
- Both SF2View (DOM) and PixiSF2View (GL) activate live sync

Architecture matches native SF2 editor 1:1:
  User edit → store → writeRAM(addr, val) → driver reads next frame → SID plays

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix channel routing: use parallel send architecture (no disconnects)

Previous approaches failed because Tone.js disconnect() is unreliable
with wrapped nodes (Channel → PanVol → Volume → Gain chain).

New approach: parallel send — don't disconnect anything. Instead:
- channel.connect(subMix) adds a parallel path alongside the existing
  channel → masterInput connection
- subMix → effect(wet=100%) → masterInput
- subMix gain = config.wet / 100 (controls send level)
- On teardown, just disconnect(subMix) — the original path was never touched

This is the safest architecture: no disconnects from existing graph,
no volume manipulation, just additive parallel connections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
